title: Configurable workflow corridor for customer-safe PM execution
corridor_story: ProductOS can show one externally shareable workflow corridor that keeps stage ownership, segment/package variation, and proof posture visible without relying on presenter narration.

scenario_baseline.label: Growth Team Baseline
scenario_baseline.summary: A product team starts with a reusable mission spine and activates overlays for package scope, buyer segment, and operating model.

canonical_stages.label: Discover
canonical_stages.headline: Normalize signal intake into one mission spine
canonical_stages.summary: Evidence from notes, support exports, and research flows into one governed discovery posture.

canonical_stages.label: Align
canonical_stages.headline: Turn proof into one customer-safe corridor story
canonical_stages.summary: Narrative, overlays, and package scope stay bounded to what the repo can prove.

canonical_stages.label: Operate
canonical_stages.headline: Route ownership transitions with watch and blocked states visible
canonical_stages.summary: The corridor keeps handoffs explicit so operators can see who moves work next and what is still bounded.

lanes.label: Signal intake
lanes.summary: Raw product and market input enters the system.

lanes.label: PM synthesis
lanes.summary: PM curates the baseline and approves corridor copy.

lanes.label: Execution control
lanes.summary: Operators monitor blockers, releases, and ownership transitions.

lanes.label: Decision trust
lanes.summary: Executives and buyers review one safe decision story.

operating_models.label: Async operating model
operating_models.summary: Keeps the evidence rail and handoff notes visible for self-serve review.

persona_views.label: Buyer
persona_views.summary: Wants confidence that ProductOS handles workflow complexity without hiding proof posture.
persona_views.goal: Understand where the workflow creates confidence and what remains bounded.

persona_views.label: Operator
persona_views.summary: Needs owner transitions and blocked states to stay explicit.
persona_views.goal: See where to intervene when the corridor shifts from approved to watch or blocked.

segment_overlays.label: Mid-market product team
segment_overlays.summary: Highlights the baseline corridor for teams that need explicit trust and packaging clarity before purchase.

package_scope.label: Corridor Pro
package_scope.summary: Includes publishable corridor generation, proof rail, and persona overlays.

hidden_by_default_rules.label: Hide internal-only workspace detail
hidden_by_default_rules.reason: Public pages cannot expose private product or customer notes.

terminal_outcomes.label: Externally shareable confidence
terminal_outcomes.summary: Customers can understand the workflow, proof posture, and tradeoffs without a narrated demo.

kpi_mappings.label: Publish confidence
kpi_mappings.summary: Corridor publish checks remain excellent across buyer and operator overlays.

proof_items.label: Structured intake evidence
proof_items.summary: The workflow is artifact-driven from reusable ProductOS inputs rather than manual page-only editing.

proof_items.label: Corridor publish gate coverage
proof_items.summary: Narrative, proof visibility, customer safety, and reading path are all scored before public release.

proof_items.label: Ownership transition observability
proof_items.summary: The corridor can show watch states for handoffs, but some customer outcome proof is still bounded.

message_claims.message: One corridor page can communicate the workflow, trust posture, and packaging story without narration.
Ownership transitions remain credible because watch states stay explicit instead of being hidden.

redaction_notes: Internal workspace references remain hidden behind customer-safe labels.

manual_copy_overrides.replacement: Turn proof into one externally credible corridor story.

audience_simplifications: Collapse low-signal internal package notes into one package card for buyers.
